updated: July 16, 2026 evening sync. Two real things landed since this morning. ONE, Jermaine answered Shuna on the Young Professionals Conference. He passed on presenting the Your Digital Footprint workshop because he will be out of the country August 4 to 11, so the $250 honorarium is off the table. The young people are still going to the field day, and he is registering the group and chaperones before Monday July 20. That same out of country window, August 4 to 11, covers all of NAACP Week, so it now shapes Week 5: give DTE Wednesday July 22 instead of August 6, and AI Ethics Day has to land Monday August 3. TWO, Connect Detroit firmed up the Payroll 2 documentation rules at 3:42 PM. The Payroll Transcript they send you is NOT acceptable documentation. What gets your 15 paid is your own timesheets, sign in sheets, or benchmark tracking sheets, showing each youth's name, the dates and times worked or benchmarks completed, and youth signatures where they apply. You bring those to pay card pickup on Thursday July 23, and remaining pay will not process until Connect Detroit has them. They sent sample timesheet and benchmark sheets to match. Everything else holds: banking and credit still open, Shantael's $1,500 unpaid, Mental Health in Week 4 and NAACP Political Education in Week 5 still need names.
headline: Week 2, Financial Literacy, is underway (opened Monday July 13). Huntington backed out of Banking 101 this year, so both the banking and credit sessions still need a partner. YNAB with Jermaine is set. DTE is back in with Jon Johnson on Work Readiness. He asked for August 4, but that is the Voter Hotline and Jermaine is out of the country August 4 to 11, so send him Wednesday July 22. Jermaine passed on the YPC workshop for the same reason, and the youth are still going to the August 5 field day, registering by Monday July 20. Wayne State has your answers, Jessica is building the July 28 schedule. CPR is approved and locked for Thursday July 30. Payroll 2 documentation firmed up July 16: the Payroll Transcript Connect Detroit sends is not acceptable, so bring your own signed sign in and benchmark sheets to pay card pickup Thursday July 23 or the pay will not process. First pay July 31.

doNow.title: Give DTE Wednesday July 22, the other options are gone
doNow.desc: Jon Johnson, Program Manager in Workforce Development at DTE, came in July 14 on Work Readiness and asked to confirm Tuesday August 4 at 12:00. You never offered August 4. Your July 10 email offered Tue 7/21, Wed 7/22 or Thu 7/23, and then Wed 8/5 or Thu 8/6. He read week of August 4 and picked the Tuesday. August 4 is the Voter Hotline, August 5 is the YPC field day, and Jermaine is now out of the country August 4 to 11, so that whole week and the August 6 option are all gone. That leaves Wednesday July 22 at noon, the only open day in Week 3 and one you already offered him. Send him July 22.

doNow.title: Confirm the YPC youth registration is submitted (workshop declined)
doNow.desc: Half done. Jermaine replied to Shuna July 16 and passed on presenting Your Digital Footprint, because he is out of the country August 4 to 11, so the $250 honorarium is off. The youth are still going to the field day on Wednesday August 5 at Huntington Place, and he said he is registering the group and chaperones before Monday July 20. That registration is first come first served and the deadline is Monday, so make sure the org, youth and chaperone registration actually went in before then.

doNow.title: Cover the banking side of Week 2 (Huntington out)
doNow.desc: Courtney Elledge at Huntington wrote back and cannot do Banking 101 this year. She offered to send financial education and banking materials for the youth to use during the week. So now both Banking 101 and the credit session still need a plan. Take her materials, and either find a fast replacement or have Jermaine run Banking 101. Week 2 is already underway, so this needs a plan now.

doNow.title: Price CPR for staff and youth council
doNow.desc: Kamilia approved the CPR and First Aid cert for all 15 youth (Natosha Gurley, RN, $12 each, about $180). She now wants the total cost to also certify staff and some youth council members. Natosha is open to more people. Get a headcount and send Kamilia the number.

doNow.title: Confirm all 15 are on the final GDYT approved list
doNow.desc: Nicole Hill's July 14 update says the Youth Enrollment Tracker now holds the final Approved for Placement list, and only youth on it can work or be paid. Youth who worked before being approved cannot be paid for that time, and GDYT will not cover unapproved youth. Check the YET and confirm all 15 of ours show Approved for Placement. Also get the worksite ready for GDYT monitor visits over the next few weeks: original work permits on hand and a first aid kit.

doNow.title: Get your Payroll 2 documentation signed for the July 23 pickup
doNow.desc: Connect Detroit firmed up the Payroll 2 rules July 16, in two emails. The 3 AM pivot said myOneFlow will not be ready and they would send a Payroll Transcript. The 3:42 PM follow up made the documentation rules clear: the Payroll Transcript they send you is NOT acceptable payroll documentation. What gets your 15 paid is your own timesheets, sign in sheets, or benchmark tracking sheets, showing each youth's name, the dates and times worked or benchmarks completed, and youth signatures where they apply. They attached sample timesheet and benchmark sheets to match. You bring that documentation to pay card pickup on Thursday July 23, and remaining pay will not process until Connect Detroit has it. So get your hand kept sign in and benchmark sheets clean and signed now.

doNow.title: Get the 15 youth into the GDYT Empower App
doNow.desc: Kelsey at Connect Detroit sent the app sign-up reminder with a youth instructions deck. The app's modules also satisfy the GDYT financial literacy requirement, and it's separate from payroll. Connect Detroit will send weekly emails showing who still needs to register, so get everyone in early.

doNow.title: Lock the rest of Week 4
doNow.desc: CPR is locked for Thursday July 30 (approved). Still need Keosha's health day (7/29 or 7/30) and Zipporah's Financial Wellness date.

doNow.title: Answer the two Week 3 virtual requests
doNow.desc: A brother and sister both emailed July 12 asking to work virtually July 20 to 29, and they are fully out July 21 to 24. That covers the in-person heart of Week 3 (Dream Car Reveal Tue 7/21, Entrepreneur Panel Thu 7/23). Reply to work out a virtual plan for those days so they stay on payroll and don't fall behind on the Capstone.

doNow.title: Confirm the two bounced families are set for the rest
doNow.desc: Two addresses bounced on the July 6 all-family email. Week 1 is done. Confirm both families by text or call that they have the schedule for the remaining weeks. One of the two also has a working gmail on the same thread.

waiting.name: Jessica Archer (Wayne State)
waiting.note: Answered July 15 at 5:14. You asked the group that morning and sent her the whole list: campus life, the law school and the professor named Ryan who got the biggest reaction, the BSMD eight year medical track, engineering and the new building, business and marketing, scholarships and internships, both asked unprompted, the Transfer Lounge, dorms, the Student Center, D9 and the black student orgs, the gym and the basketball courts, the game room, the Wright Museum, food, parking. Plus your two: what it takes to get in and what it costs, and a VP or dean on how to actually use a campus and its resources. You offered roster, forms and waivers same day. Now she builds the July 28 schedule and confirms lunch.

waiting.name: GDYT payroll + pay cards
waiting.note: Payroll 2 firmed up July 16 in two emails from Nicole Hill. The 3 AM pivot: myOneFlow will not be ready, so there is no youth or supervisor action in the system. The 3:42 PM follow up set the documentation rules: the Payroll Transcript Connect Detroit sends is NOT acceptable documentation. What counts is your own timesheets, sign in sheets, or benchmark tracking sheets, with each youth's name, dates and times worked or benchmarks completed, and youth signatures where they apply. They attached sample timesheet and benchmark sheets. Remaining pay will not process until Connect Detroit has that documentation. Still true from the July 14 update: only youth on the final Approved for Placement list in the Youth Enrollment Tracker can be paid, so confirm all 15 are on it (see Do Now), and GDYT monitors will visit the worksite to check safety, supervision, original work permits and a first aid kit. Our group is Payroll 2, first pay July 31. Pay card pickup is Thursday July 23, by Calendly appointment only, and you must bring the signed sign in and benchmark sheets or the cards are not released.
